engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 207 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 207 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.4L Diesel
entries.history: The 8HZ powered the entry-level 207 HDi, offering exceptional fuel economy but requiring strict timing belt intervals to avoid catastrophic failure.
compatibleVariants: 1.4 HDi Active, 1.4 HDi Access, 1.4 HDi Sport, 1.4 HDi SW
commonFailures: Head gasket failure due to aluminium block warping under high heat
EGR valve sticking causing rough idle and limp mode
Timing belt tensioner wear if service intervals missed
entries.cta: Get quotes for 8HZ Peugeot 207 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The 9HX delivered 90hp across the mid-range HDi trims, becoming the UK’s volume diesel choice for low-tax commuters and fleet operators.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi 90 Active, 1.6 HDi 90 Sport, 1.6 HDi 90 GT, 1.6 HDi 90 SW
commonFailures: Turbo actuator failure causing loss of boost power
Diesel injector coding drift leading to rough running
Crankshaft bearing wear beyond 90k miles on poorly maintained units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HX Peugeot 207 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The uprated 9HZ (110hp) brought stronger performance to GT and SW trims, though higher cylinder pressures increased DPF and turbo stress.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 HDi 110 Sport, 1.6 HDi 110 GT, 1.6 HDi 110 SW, 1.6 HDi 110 CC
commonFailures: DPF blockage on urban driving cycles triggering anti-pollution faults

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starting
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HZ Peugeot 207 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Developed with BMW, the EP3 offered smooth, high-revving performance for the entry-level 207 petrol range, though early units suffered from oil consumption.
compatibleVariants: 1.4 VTi Active, 1.4 VTi Sport, 1.4 VTi GT, 1.4 VTi SW
commonFailures: Piston ring wear causing high oil consumption and smoke
Ignition coil pack failure under thermal stress
Camshaft adjuster solenoid failure triggering engine light
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP3 Peugeot 207 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The EP6 replaced the older TU series, delivering 120hp and refined power delivery, but introduced the notorious timing chain design.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 VTi Active, 1.6 VTi Sport, 1.6 VTi GT, 1.6 VTi SW, 1.6 VTi CC
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing chain rattle and jump
Water pump bearing seizure leading to overheating
VANOS solenoid clogging affecting valve timing
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6 Peugeot 207 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: The turbocharged Prince variant powered the THP and RC models, delivering 150-175hp with direct injection but requiring meticulous maintenance.
compatibleVariants: 1.6 THP 150 GT, 1.6 THP 150 Sport, 1.6 THP RC, 1.6 THP RC GTi

entries.title: pressure fuel pump (HPFP) internal failure under boost
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6DT Peugeot 207 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 207 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Peugeot 207 Timing Chain Failure - Why EP6 Petrol Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain tensioner loses hydraulic pressure, allowing the chain to stretch and skip teeth on the cam sprockets.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New chain, tensioner, guides and seals
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable for 60k+ miles if caught before jump

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt EP6 with upgraded tensioner

problems.recommendation: If you hear a cold-start rattle above 50k miles, address the chain immediately. Once the chain jumps, valve damage is almost guaranteed, making full replacement the only economical option on a 207 valued under £2,500.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP6 engine prices for your Peugeot 207

problems.h4: Peugeot 207 Turbo Failure - Why 1.6 HDi Engines Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: Oil starvation and carbon buildup on the turbo actuator arm cause sticking vanes and eventual compressor blade fatigue.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo removal, actuator calibration, carbon clean
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix only if blades are undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full DV6 rebuild with upgraded turbo and oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates downstream bearing damage from metal shards

problems.recommendation: On high-mileage 207 HDi units, a failing turbo usually indicates broader oil circulation issues. Replacing just the turbo rarely prevents recurrence; a fully rebuilt engine with corrected oil galleries offers the only long-term solution.
problems.cta: Find reconditioned 9HZ Peugeot 207 engines with warranty

problems.h4: Peugeot 207 Injector Failure - Fuel Delivery Issues on HDi Units
problems.rootCause: Piezo injector seals degrade under high thermal load, allowing combustion gases to leak and carbonise around the injector seat.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, test, replace faulty injectors, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if only one injector has failed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt DV6 with fresh injector harness and seals
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when multiple injectors fail or head seats are damaged

problems.recommendation: If you experience rough idle and black smoke after 80k miles, get injector coding checked first. However, if cylinder head damage is confirmed from gas leakage, a rebuilt engine is significantly cheaper than head milling plus four new injectors.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 207 diesel injector and engine replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot 207 DPF Blockage - Anti-Pollution Fault on HDi Models
problems.rootCause: Frequent short trips prevent DPF regeneration cycles, causing soot and oil ash to solidify in the filter substrate.

repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if filter isn’t physically cracked

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DV6 with DPF delete or EGR/DPF bypass kit
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for urban drivers or vehicles with chronic regeneration failure

problems.recommendation: DPF blockage on the 207 rarely happens in isolation-it usually points to EGR faults or turbo oil burn. For city-dwelling owners, a rebuilt engine paired with a compliant exhaust modification offers better long-term reliability than repeated DPF replacements.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot 207 HDi engine prices with DPF solutions

problems.h4: Peugeot 207 HPFP Failure - High-Pressure Fuel Pump on THP Models
problems.rootCause: The Bosch CP3/4 internal cam follower wears prematurely, causing metal contamination to circulate through the high-pressure fuel rail and injectors.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace pump, flush fuel lines, replace inline filter
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before metal debris reaches injectors

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full EP6DT rebuild with upgraded HPFP and cleaned rail
repairOptions.longevity: Essential when metallic particles have contaminated the combustion chambers

problems.recommendation: HPFP failure on the THP is catastrophic if delayed. If diagnostic codes show fuel rail pressure deviation alongside metallic fuel, budget for a full engine replacement immediately-repairing injectors alone will not prevent repeat failure.
problems.cta: Find specialist Peugeot 207 THP 175 rebuilt engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 207
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot 207’s engine issues are well-documented but entirely solvable when you compare transparent pricing from independent specialists rather than paying main dealer premiums. EngineMarket connects you directly to vetted UK workshops that supply rebuilt units with upgraded components, bench testing, and a 12-month warranty as standard. Nationwide delivery and 24-48 hour turnaround mean your 207 spends less time off the road and more time back in your hands. Compare prices today and secure a reliable replacement without the write-off cost.

engineTypes.h2: Peugeot 207 Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type is available through our UK network with realistic price ranges based on your Peugeot 207 variant, engine code, and budget.

types.title: Peugeot 207 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Full strip, clean, measure and rebuild with new gaskets, seals and worn parts replaced to factory tolerances.
types.frontDescription: Full strip, clean, measure and rebuild with new gaskets, seals and worn parts replaced to factory tolerances.
types.frontDisclaimer: Best balance of cost and reliability for high-mileage units.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned units undergo complete disassembly, block honing, and head resurfacing to restore original compression. Upgraded timing tensioners and seals address known 207 failure points before reassembly. Bench-tested for oil pressure and compression.
backBullets: Ideal for DV6 and EP6 owners seeking OEM reliability

types.title: Peugeot 207 Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Core engine stripped and rebuilt with selective component replacement where wear exceeds manufacturer specifications.
types.frontDescription: Core engine stripped and rebuilt with selective component replacement where wear exceeds manufacturer specifications.
types.frontDisclaimer: Most popular choice for budget-conscious owners.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt engines focus on critical wear areas like piston rings, bearings and chain tensioners. Less comprehensive than recon but fully functional for standard road use on the 207 platform.
backBullets: Cost-effective solution for VTi and HDi models
Fully tested and road-ready on delivery

types.title: Peugeot 207 Used Engines
types.description: Direct removal from low-mileage donor vehicles, tested for compression, oil pressure and clean diagnostics.
types.frontDescription: Direct removal from low-mileage donor vehicles, tested for compression, oil pressure and clean diagnostics.
types.frontDisclaimer: Lowest upfront cost; warranty depends on supplier.
types.backDescription: Used engines are sourced from verified breaker networks and inspected for structural integrity. Ideal for older 207 models where full rebuild costs approach vehicle value.
backBullets: Fastest availability for common 9HX and 8HZ codes
Requires professional installation and ECU adaptation

types.title: Peugeot 207 Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Complete factory-standard rebuild to original OEM blueprints using new and reconditioned components.
types.frontDescription: Complete factory-standard rebuild to original OEM blueprints using new and reconditioned components.
types.frontDisclaimer: Highest specification; matches new engine performance.
types.backDescription: Remanufactured units are engineered to PSA factory tolerances with all consumables replaced. Perfect for RC and THP owners demanding maximum longevity and power retention.
backBullets: Restores original 175hp output on THP units
Includes upgraded HPFP on turbo petrol codes

types.title: Peugeot 207 Refurbished Engines
types.description: Minor repair and cleaning of functional core engines with seal replacement and diagnostic clearance.
types.frontDescription: Minor repair and cleaning of functional core engines with seal replacement and diagnostic clearance.
types.frontDisclaimer: Suitable for minor wear; not for catastrophic failures.
types.backDescription: Refurbished engines undergo light machining and gasket replacement to clear minor oil leaks and restore compression. Best for low-mileage 1.4 VTi units needing a refresh.
backBullets: Limited warranty coverage on core block

types.title: Peugeot 207 Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Complete package covering engine supply, professional installation, fluid top-up and post-fit diagnostics.
types.frontDescription: Complete package covering engine supply, professional installation, fluid top-up and post-fit diagnostics.
types.frontDisclaimer: Removes DIY stress; includes full labour guarantee.
types.backDescription: Supply and fit packages handle timing alignment, ECU coding, DPF reset and road testing. Specialists familiar with 207 chassis points ensure correct mounting and ancillary routing.
backBullets: Includes all seals, belts and cooling components
Single point of contact for parts and labour

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot 207 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot 207 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you compare replacement options.

items.title: Peugeot 207 1.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.4L DV4 unit formed the entry-level diesel foundation of the 207 range, prioritising exceptional fuel economy and low insurance groups over outright performance. It was paired exclusively with 5-speed manual gearboxes and fitted to Access, Active and basic Sport trims across the early generations. Strict adherence to timing belt intervals was critical to prevent catastrophic internal interference.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 207 1.4L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 207 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L DV6 became the backbone of the 207 diesel lineup, offering strong mid-range torque for SW estates and GT trims while maintaining competitive road tax bands. Later models introduced the 110hp 9HZ variant, which required more robust turbo and fuel system calibration. Urban driving cycles frequently accelerated DPF and turbo actuator degradation on higher-output tunes.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 207 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 207 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L EP3 Prince petrol engine delivered a smooth, high-revving character suited to city and commuter driving, replacing the older TU series across the 2007 facelift. It featured variable valve timing and a compact aluminium block, though early production runs suffered from oil consumption and coil pack sensitivity. Proper oil specification and cooling system maintenance significantly extended service life.
commonFailurePoints: Piston ring wear causing oil burn

items.title: Peugeot 207 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L EP6 and EP6DT variants powered the performance end of the 207 range, spanning naturally aspirated VTi models and the turbocharged THP/RC badges. Direct injection and twin-scroll turbocharging on the DT variant delivered strong acceleration but introduced complex high-pressure fuel system maintenance requirements. Timing chain tensioner design flaws remain the most documented replacement trigger across both tunes.

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Peugeot 207 needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Peugeot 207 specialists.

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 207 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 207 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The initial UK launch focused on economical 1.4L and 1.6L diesel units, establishing the 207 as a low-cost commuter with strong tax band positioning.
keyChanges: Introduction of DV4 1.4 HDi and DV6 1.6 HDi engines
TU1.4 and TU1.6 carryover petrol units from 206 range
SW estate variant announced with reinforced chassis
knownFor: Robust but dated TU-series petrol architecture
Early DPF calibration issues on post-2007 models
High UK sales volume for fleet and first-time buyers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2007 Peugeot 207 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 207 1.4 HDi engine replacement • Peugeot 207 TU5 timing belt • Peugeot 207 9HX turbo cost

years.description: PSA/BMW’s Prince petrol family replaced outdated TU units, bringing 120hp VTi and 150-175hp turbo variants that redefined 207 performance.
keyChanges: EP3 1.4L and EP6 1.6L petrol engines launched
1.6 THP 150/175 turbo models introduced for GT and RC badges
CC convertible launched with reinforced engine mounts
knownFor: Smooth high-revving power delivery and direct injection
Early timing chain tensioner design flaws emerge
HPFP reliability concerns on urban driving cycles
years.cta: Find rebuilt EP6 Peugeot 207 engines for your model year
years.ticker: Peugeot 207 timing chain rattle • Peugeot 207 THP engine failure • Peugeot 207 EP6 replacement cost

years.description: Mid-cycle updates focused on EGR tuning, DPF regeneration logic and minor engine calibration tweaks to meet Euro 5 standards without hardware changes.
keyChanges: Revised EGR valve calibration on 1.6 HDi 110 units
Improved DPF regeneration software for urban 207s
1.4 VTi oil consumption fixes via revised piston rings
knownFor: Improved cold-start reliability on Prince petrol engines
DPF warning lights reduced but urban blockage persists
Mature platform with wide specialist coverage

years.description: The final UK production years maintained stable engine line-ups as manufacturing transitioned to the 208, leaving a mature replacement market for all 207 variants.
keyChanges: No major hardware updates; focus on parts availability
RC and GT variants phased out in UK market
SW models remain popular with specialist rebuilders
knownFor: Fully documented failure patterns and aftermarket support
Affordable rebuilt engine market due to high parts volume
Strong resale value for clean, low-mileage examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2014 Peugeot 207 replacement engines

faq.h2: Peugeot 207 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Peugeot 207?
items.answer: Yes, replacing the engine is highly cost-effective for a 207 valued between £1,200-£3,000, especially when opting for a rebuilt unit with warranty instead of scrapping. The 207 platform is robust, and engine replacement typically costs less than purchasing a comparable used vehicle.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines cost £850-£1,800 depending on fuel type
Scrapping rarely covers outstanding finance or replacement purchase
UK specialists offer 24-48 hour fitting turnaround
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 207 engine replacement prices today

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Peugeot 207 engine?
items.answer: UK replacement costs range from £650 for used units to £2,200 for fully rebuilt turbo petrol engines, with labour adding £450-£900 depending on workshop location. Rebuilt 1.6 HDi units typically sit around £1,400-£1,800 including standard supply-only pricing.
keyPoints: Used engines start at £650 supply-only
Rebuilt DV6 and EP6 units range £1,200-£1,950
Labour adds £450-£900 for full installation
items.cta: Get tailored Peugeot 207 engine quotes now

items.question: What causes Peugeot 207 timing chain failure?
items.answer: The rear-mounted hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing the chain to stretch and potentially skip cam timing, particularly on EP6 petrol engines above 60k miles. Ignoring cold-start rattling usually leads to bent valves and catastrophic internal damage.
keyPoints: Tensioner hydraulic seals degrade with mileage
Cold rattle indicates immediate inspection need
Rebuilt units include upgraded tensioner kits
items.cta: Find rebuilt EP6 engines for your Peugeot 207

items.question: Why do Peugeot 207 diesel engines lose power?
items.answer: Turbo actuator sticking, DPF blockage, or injector coding drift are the primary culprits behind power loss on 1.6 HDi 207 units, often triggered by urban driving or poor fuel quality. Diagnostic scanning usually identifies whether boost pressure or fuel delivery is restricted.
keyPoints: Actuator arm carbon buildup restricts vanes
DPF regeneration failure triggers limp mode
Injector seal leaks cause combustion pressure loss
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 207 HDi engine replacement costs

items.question: Are used Peugeot 207 engines reliable?
items.answer: Used engines can be reliable if sourced from verified breakers with compression and oil pressure tests, but they lack the upgraded components that address known 207 failure points like timing tensioners. Always verify service history and request a minimum 3-month warranty before fitting.
keyPoints: Breaker-sourced units require pre-installation inspection
Missing upgraded tensioners increases future risk
Rebuilt units include full seal and bearing replacement

items.question: Should I buy petrol or diesel for a Peugeot 207?
items.answer: Choose diesel if you regularly exceed 10,000 motorway miles annually for superior fuel economy, but opt for petrol if you predominantly drive short urban trips to avoid DPF regeneration failures. Both fuel types have well-documented failure patterns and established UK replacement networks.
keyPoints: Diesel excels on long-distance motorway commuting
Petrol avoids complex DPF and EGR maintenance
Both platforms share similar rebuild pricing
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 207 petrol vs diesel engines

items.question: Can I fit a Peugeot 207 engine myself?
items.answer: DIY fitting is possible for experienced mechanics with engine hoists and torque wrenches, but ECU coding, DPF reset and ancillary routing require specialist diagnostic equipment. Professional supply-and-fit packages typically cost £450-£900 and include full system validation.
keyPoints: ECU adaptation requires proprietary diagnostic tools
Timing alignment errors cause immediate failure
Specialist fitting includes coolant and EGR calibration
items.cta: Find Peugeot 207 engine supply & fit deals

items.question: What warranty comes with a Peugeot 207 replacement engine?
items.answer: UK specialists typically offer 12-month unlimited mileage warranties on rebuilt and reconditioned units, while used engines usually carry 3-6 month limited coverage. Warranty terms exclude improper installation, cooling system neglect, and unauthorised tuning modifications.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include 12-month standard coverage
Used units typically offer 90-180 day terms
Warranty voided by DIY installation errors
items.cta: View Peugeot 207 engine warranty terms today

items.question: Where do I find my Peugeot 207 engine code?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the block near the oil filter housing or on the VIN plate under the bonnet, typically appearing as 8HZ, 9HX, EP6 or similar. Cross-referencing this code with your V5C registration document ensures exact compatibility before ordering replacement parts.
keyPoints: Located near oil filter or timing belt cover
Matches V5C registration section D.2
Essential for correct turbo and ECU mapping
items.cta: Identify your Peugeot 207 engine code instantly

items.question: How long does a Peugeot 207 engine last?
items.answer: With proper servicing and correct oil specifications, 207 engines routinely exceed 120,000-150,000 miles before major internal wear requires replacement. Neglected cooling systems, missed timing intervals, and poor fuel quality significantly reduce lifespan on both petrol and diesel variants.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes prevent chain and bearing wear
Cooling system flushes avoid head gasket stress
Quality fuel reduces HPFP and injector degradation
